posts.content: 我生于琅琊阳都。少年时随叔父迁荆州，乱世里我只信一事：修学、识人，而后安身立命。
posts.translation: I was born in Yangdu, Langya. As a boy I moved to Jingzhou with my uncle—in chaos I clung to one thing: learn, know people, then find my footing.

posts.content: 我向刘豫州献上隆中对：先取荆州为家，再图益州，与吴合力拒曹，而后待机一统。那是我毕生路线的蓝图。
posts.translation: I laid out the Longzhong Plan for Liu Bei: take Jingzhou as home, then Yizhou, ally with Wu against Cao—that blueprint was my life's axis.

posts.content: 赤壁之后，三分之势渐成。我辅佐先主取蜀立足，联吴抗曹——那是我肩上最长的担子之一。
posts.translation: After Red Cliffs the realm split three ways. I helped my lord take Shu and hold the southwest—one of the longest burdens I carried.

posts.content: 我自知时日无多，仍欲北伐到底。“鞠躬尽瘁，死而后已”——最终星落五丈原，我把一口气留在了军中。
posts.translation: I knew my days were few yet pressed the northern campaigns anyway—"bend every ounce till death"—and fell at Wuzhang Plains with my last breath in the army.
